reviews.review_description: This is one of my favorite games ever! I like it a lot more than any rust type game on VR! And to those who say the community is toxic, literally everyone’s trying to kill each other in this game, that’s why it’s so fun! And the community ￼ is really not that bad. A thing I would really appreciate being added would be private lobbies. That would make the game very fun to play with friends! And another thing would be drivable helicopters. Also a way to skip the tutorial.  Thanks!￼

This game is the better rust vr in many ways, the only reason you see one star reviews is because the start for grim is harder, and that’s the biggest difference from strayed. The only thing strayed had going for it the biomes and vehicles, but that’s it; besides that grim had better graphics, controls, progression system, and npc’s. Stop clowning on grim, and just get better for doing strayed.

reviews.review_title: ABSOLUTE BEST pvp/ survival GAME ON VR
reviews.review_description:
If you're looking for a VR game that actually makes your heart race, get GRIM.

It's basically Rust in VR, but somehow even more immersive. Every trip outside your base feels like an adventure. One minute you're scavenging supplies, the next you're in a gunfight, escaping bears or running into random players you have no idea whether to trust lol.

The world feels alive. The survival mechanics are fun without being overwhelming, base building is addictive, and the player interactions create stories you'll remember long after you log off. (Trust me on that one) Some of my favorite gaming moments ever have come from this game.

What really makes GRIM special is that no two days are the same. You can spend hours exploring, making allies, fighting enemies, raiding, farming, grubbing, running monuments like the dark underground military tunnels fighting scientists, or just surviving against the odds.

Honestly, GRIM deserves way more players than it has right now. If you've ever wanted to feel like you're actually inside a survival game instead of just controlling a character, this is it.

Ive been playing strayed scince day 1 and i decided to get grim to test it out and holy this is awfull. Im sorry to the devs of grim but strayed is WAYYYYYYYY better. I started at the tutorial (1hr long btw) and i could tell it was awful. The graphics,horrible,the pvp,awful,the overall atmosphere,awful,and finally collecting resources it generally impossible. Please rather fix ur game or refund me becase i wasted my money.

If you are reading this wanting to get or even play grim if you do or havent got it. Grim is way better than strayed by a mile there is so much more and fun stuff to do. Pls get the game bc the servers avrage about 20 to 17 ppl. If you guys could play pls do with a friend 100x better. Must get

developer_response.body:
Hey NRMDManjiro.sano,
While a single player mode is not something we can do (a lot of Grim is handled by the server), we do plan on adding server hosting. That will allow you and your friends to host your own server and protect it with a password.

Once we get there, you will be able to tweak the server settings too.

reviews.review_title: Continues to improve and is a lot of fun
reviews.review_description: Kudos to the developer for continuing to add features and fix bugs.   It's truly "Rust" in VR and is a ton of fun to explore,  loot and engaged in some PVP with other players in the map.   Building has a bit of a learning curve and I do wish the tutorial was skippable, minor gripes though!   Go buy it and test your resolve!

developer_response.body:
Thank you, AlphaPuppet!

There's a lot more to come! I hope you enjoy.

reviews.review_title: Not a lot of people play...
reviews.review_description: This is my one problem with the game. Not a lot of people play it anymore. I feel like they just need a big update to bring players back. Mars again maybe? Or maybe new mechanics that could change the game, or new guns and armor? Just new stuff. But that's my only problem with the game tbh. Like my friend and I got on to try the game and there were 4 people on a server.. yes, 4. Good game tho.

developer_response.body:
Hey M_P_ejjeffry,
Grim is developed by a single person so it is hard to make massive updates. With that said, the player base has been growing since we added Earth instead of Mars. Bringing Mars back (two maps) would divide the players.

In the past 3 months, we added the patrol helicopter, new build parts, vehicles (testing), horses and more! We have plans to add more in the coming months too.

We are hoping it will grow the player base.
Thank you for the review!

developer_response.body:
Sorry to hear that, Foxwrth. 
We moved  the game from Mars to Earth because that is where it started initially (pre-early access). Based on our community, Mars made it hard with the oxygen system and biomes focused gameplay loop. We understand that some players enjoyed Mars and we hope you will enjoy what we've been working on to make Earth a better user experience.

As a Strayed player, it was difficult to get into Grim. Once I learned the basics, Grim grew on me. I now primarily play Grim. 

It is challenging. You will get raided and lose all your stuff many times. Build a metal base with metal doors asap. This prevents people from eco raiding. Learn all the monuments. Once you're able to do Military Tunnels, you're in the endgame. After you can do tunnels with relative ease, you'll be rich (until you're raided). Solo is hard. Try to join a team.

I appreciate the game; however, it is apparent that the community is facing challenges that detract from the overall experience. A couple of potential improvements could enhance player engagement significantly. 

Firstly, introducing a system where the game world does not reset every two weeks could provide a more stable environment, particularly for solo players. Instead of resetting the entire world, perhaps you could consider a model where group participants reset every other week while allowing solo players to retain their progress.

Additionally, implementing a feature that enables players to keep their blueprints and other acquired assets would alleviate the frustration of starting over from scratch after each reset. This change could help maintain player interest and commitment to the game.

Lastly, I suggest exploring the option of offering players the ability to purchase their own worlds for hosting dedicated servers at a monthly fee. Such enhancements have the potential to greatly improve the player experience and encourage long-term engagement. Many solo players may find it disheartening to consistently restart every two weeks, and addressing this concern could significantly increase overall satisfaction with the game.

developer_response.body:
Hey Official.Country.Lunatic,
Thank you for the review!

We did experiment with longer and shorter wipes. The shorter wipes surely was not long enough, and the longer wipes had players losing interest due to some teams dominating the server and clearing any competition around. It allowed players to build massive fortress and hurt performances too. But I will provide a detail you might enjoy when covering your last point!

Blueprints reset is also something that can be fine tuned in order to enhance the player experience. It is something that we played around with and settled on what we currently have, but we are open to suggestions! Join the discord at discord.gg/GrimVR to provide us more ideas and see how the community react to it.

Lastly, the idea of player hosted servers is definitely on the table. That would allow them to fully control the wipe cycle and decide what is best for them.

I hope my answers provided you a glimpse in the future that you appreciate!
-Mikael
